day_backups.label: Hanoi AM → Sapa PM

best_window.note: No clear window — best available slot only

backup_plan.name: Indoor cafe crawl (fog/rain)
backup_plan.note: The Hill Station, Leopard Cat Cafe — mountain views from inside

swap_suggestion.problem: July = peak fog season. Visibility near zero after 2PM. Fansipan summit often in clouds.
swap_suggestion.alternative: Stay extra day in Hanoi — day trip to Ninh Binh (Trang An boat ride, rain or shine)
swap_suggestion.alternative2: Swap Sapa days for Phong Nha caves (indoor cave tours, weather-proof)
swap_suggestion.cost_impact: Bus to Ninh Binh ₹600 vs Sapa bus ₹1,050. Save ₹450
swap_suggestion.verdict: Keep Sapa — but use backup indoor activities. Fog is part of the experience. Only swap if 3+ consecutive days show >80% rain.

day_backups.recommendation: ⚠️ High weather risk (99% rain, 1h fog). Keep original plan but prepare to switch to indoor alternatives. Best window: 06:00-06:00.

day_backups.label: Day Trip — Highest Risk Day

backup_plan.name: Ceramic Mural + Long Bien Bridge walk
Old Quarter food crawl (covered)
backup_plan.note: Bun Cha, pho, egg coffee — all indoor seating

swap_suggestion.problem: Highest risk day. Cruises cancel if wind > 25km/h or typhoon warning.
swap_suggestion.alternative: Ninh Binh — 'Halong Bay on land' (boat ride through limestone karsts, rain or shine)
swap_suggestion.alternative2: Hanoi full museum day + Train Street + water puppet
swap_suggestion.cost_impact: Ninh Binh tour ₹1,200 vs Halong cruise ₹2,000. Save ₹800
swap_suggestion.verdict: Check windy.com 3 days before. If wind > 25km/h or storm warning → switch to Ninh Binh immediately.

info.name: Phong Nha
info.why: World's largest cave systems. UNESCO national park. Paradise Cave (31km long). Dark Cave zip + mud bath. PERFECT rain backup — caves are INDOORS.
highlights: Paradise Cave (₹700) — wood walkway, dry, stunning
Dark Cave adventure (₹1,400) — zip + mud bath

info.name: Da Nang
info.why: Central Vietnam's best beach city. My Khe Beach (Forbes top 10). Marble Mountains. Ba Na Hills (French village + cable car). Dragon Bridge breathes fire Sat/Sun.

info.name: Hoi An
info.why: UNESCO ancient town. Lantern-lit streets. Tailor-made clothes (24hr). Best food in Vietnam per many chefs. An Bang beach. Cooking classes.
highlights: Ancient Town (FREE to walk, ₹280 ticket for 5 sites)
Cao Lau noodles (only in Hoi An)

info.name: Hue
info.why: Imperial city. Citadel + Forbidden City. Royal tombs. Perfume River. Cheapest city in Vietnam for food. Rich history.

info.name: Nha Trang
info.why: Beach resort city. VinWonders Nha Trang (bigger than Phu Quoc). Mud baths. Snorkeling islands. Cheapest beer in Vietnam. Great nightlife.

info.name: Dalat
info.why: Mountain town like Sapa but DRIER in July. French colonial architecture. Waterfalls, flower gardens, crazy house. Pine forests. Cool weather (18-22°C).

swap_recommendations.cost_impact: Flight to Phong Nha instead of Sapa bus. Similar cost ₹1,500-2,500.
swap_recommendations.reasoning: Sapa avg rain: 94%. Phong Nha avg rain: 50%. Phong Nha is 44% drier during your trip.
swap_recommendations.what_you_get: World's largest cave systems. UNESCO national park. Paradise Cave (31km long). Dark Cave zip + mud bath. PERFECT rain backup — caves are INDOORS.
highlights: Paradise Cave (₹700) — wood walkway, dry, stunning
Dark Cave adventure (₹1,400) — zip + mud bath

reorder_suggestion.current_order: HCMC → Phu Quoc → Hanoi → Sapa → Hanoi
reorder_suggestion.issue: Both Phu Quoc (monsoon) and Sapa (fog season) are in the WETTEST period. Central Vietnam is drier in July.
reorder_suggestion.suggested_reorder: HCMC (2 days) → Da Nang/Hoi An (3 days, DRIEST) → Hanoi (2 days) → Sapa (2 days) → Hanoi departure
reorder_suggestion.why: Central Vietnam (Da Nang/Hoi An) has the BEST weather in July. By going there during Jun 30-Jul 3 (instead of Phu Quoc), you get the driest window.
reorder_suggestion.cost_impact: Same number of flights. Da Nang flights ₹1,500-2,500 (same as Phu Quoc). Hoi An is cheaper than Phu Quoc (₹1,000/day vs ₹1,500/day).
reorder_suggestion.what_you_lose: VinWonders Phu Quoc, 4-island tour, cable car
reorder_suggestion.what_you_gain: Ba Na Hills Golden Bridge, Hoi An ancient town, My Khe Beach, Cao Lau noodles, tailor shopping — ALL in better weather

Da Nang.why: Central Vietnam's best beach city. My Khe Beach (Forbes top 10). Marble Mountains. Ba Na Hills (French village + cable car). Dragon Bridge breathes fire Sat/Sun.

Hoi An.why: UNESCO ancient town. Lantern-lit streets. Tailor-made clothes (24hr). Best food in Vietnam per many chefs. An Bang beach. Cooking classes.
highlights: Ancient Town (FREE to walk, ₹280 ticket for 5 sites)
Cao Lau noodles (only in Hoi An)

Hue.why: Imperial city. Citadel + Forbidden City. Royal tombs. Perfume River. Cheapest city in Vietnam for food. Rich history.

highlights: Paradise Cave (₹700) — wood walkway, dry, stunning
Dark Cave adventure (₹1,400) — zip + mud bath

Dalat.why: Mountain town like Sapa but DRIER in July. French colonial architecture. Waterfalls, flower gardens, crazy house. Pine forests. Cool weather (18-22°C).
